name: Solute carrier family 6 member 4
summary: SLC6A4 encodes the serotonin transporter (5-HTT), which reuptakes serotonin from the synaptic cleft and terminates serotonergic signaling. The promoter polymorphism 5-HTTLPR (short vs long) modulates transporter expression and has been extensively studied in depression, stress response, and suicidal behavior. Short allele carriers show exaggerated amygdala reactivity and may be more vulnerable to depression following adversity. SLC6A4 is the target of SSRIs. Expression is highest in raphe nuclei, platelets, and placenta. Genetic variation influences SSRI response. The gene-denvironment interaction (5-HTTLPR × stress → depression) has been debated but remains influential in psychiatric genetics.

regulatory_notes.promoter_activity: 5-HTTLPR length polymorphism affects transcriptional efficiency; short allele reduces expression.
regulatory_notes.methylation_sensitivity: SLC6A4 promoter methylation correlates with stress exposure and depression.

mechanistic_hypotheses.hypothesis: Short 5-HTTLPR allele reduces SLC6A4 expression and serotonin reuptake; under psychosocial stress, reduced extracellular serotonin clearance may amplify stress reactivity and predispose to depression.
mechanistic_hypotheses.supporting_evidence: Meta-analyses support G×E; short allele associated with amygdala reactivity and stress-related depression in some studies.

references.title: Influence of life stress on depression: moderation by a polymorphism in the 5-HTT gene
